title: AI as Your Teaching Assistant
subtitle: A Guide for South African Educators
description: Learn to use AI tools as your personal teaching assistant. Save time, create better lessons, and support every learner — at your own pace, in your own time.

modules.title: Hello, AI!
modules.subtitle: Welcome to Your Digital Teaching Assistant
modules.description: Demystify AI so you feel curious rather than worried. Discover what AI can and cannot do, and meet the two tools you will use throughout this course.

lessons.title: What Is AI, Really?
objectives: Define AI in plain language
Explain that AI is a tool, not a replacement for teachers
Bust three common myths about AI

lessons.title: Meet Gemini — Your AI Writing Partner
objectives: Understand what Google Gemini is and what it does best
Navigate the Gemini interface confidently
Know when to use Gemini vs other tools

lessons.title: Meet NotebookLM — Your AI Research Partner
objectives: Understand what NotebookLM is and how it differs from Gemini
Upload your first document to NotebookLM
Know when to use NotebookLM vs Gemini

lessons.title: AI Is a Tool — You Are Still the Expert
objectives: Articulate the role of the teacher vs the role of AI
Feel empowered and curious about AI rather than threatened

modules.title: The Art of the Ask
modules.subtitle: How to Talk to AI
modules.description: Learn the PARTS framework for writing prompts that get you exactly what you need. Master the skill that makes AI 10x more useful.

lessons.title: Why Your Question Matters
objectives: Understand why vague prompts produce vague results
Adopt the mindset of a director giving clear instructions

lessons.title: P is for Persona
objectives: Use the Persona element to give AI a role and communication style
Write a persona for your own subject and grade

lessons.title: A is for Aim
objectives: State the purpose of a prompt precisely
Match the aim verb to the task type

lessons.title: R is for Recipients
objectives: Specify your audience in prompts to control language level
Include South African cultural context in recipient details

lessons.title: T + S — Theme and Structure
objectives: Apply Theme and Structure to complete a full PARTS prompt
Build and copy a complete PARTS prompt for your own class

modules.title: Save Your Sundays
modules.subtitle: AI for Planning and Administration
modules.description: Reclaim your evenings and weekends. Use AI to handle lesson planning, assessments, parent communication, and differentiation in a fraction of the time.

lessons.title: Lesson Planning with AI
objectives: Use Gemini to draft a CAPS-aligned lesson plan structure
Know what to check and edit before using an AI-generated plan

lessons.title: Rubrics, Assessments, and Marking Grids
objectives: Generate assessment rubrics and memoranda using AI
Adapt AI-generated assessments to specific CAPS requirements

lessons.title: Parent and School Communication
objectives: Draft newsletters, permission letters, and parent messages faster
Maintain a professional tone while saving time

lessons.title: Differentiation — Adapting Materials for Every Learner
objectives: Use AI to quickly create multiple versions of the same material at different levels
Support learners with diverse needs using AI-assisted adaptation

modules.title: Bring Your Lessons Alive
modules.subtitle: AI for Creative Teaching
modules.description: Design more engaging and creative learning experiences. Use AI to generate compelling lesson hooks, South African analogies, and cross-curricular activities.

lessons.title: Starter Activities and Lesson Hooks
objectives: Generate engaging lesson openers that connect to SA learners' lives
Reduce time spent brainstorming lesson starters

lessons.title: Explanations, Analogies, and Storytelling
objectives: Ask AI to explain complex concepts using local South African analogies
Use storytelling to make abstract content concrete

lessons.title: Using NotebookLM for Subject Research
objectives: Upload curriculum documents to NotebookLM and extract structured summaries
Create study guides and question banks from source material

lessons.title: Cross-Curricular and Special Event Planning
objectives: Plan creative special events and theme days with AI assistance
Design cross-curricular projects for South African observances

modules.title: AI in Your Pocket
modules.subtitle: Mobile and Low-Connectivity Use
modules.description: Make AI work for you regardless of load shedding or data constraints. Learn mobile-first strategies and smart workarounds for the South African context.

lessons.title: Using AI on Mobile Devices
objectives: Access and use Gemini on Android and iPhone
Use voice input for hands-free prompt entry

lessons.title: Load Shedding Strategies
objectives: Plan ahead for load shedding so AI tools are ready when you are
Build a bank of saved AI outputs for offline use

lessons.title: Data and Cost Awareness
objectives: Understand the data costs of AI tools
Use free tiers effectively without exceeding your data budget

modules.title: Trust But Verify
modules.subtitle: Responsible AI Use in Schools
modules.description: Use AI ethically and safely. Understand its limitations, protect learner privacy under POPIA, and navigate academic integrity in the AI age.

lessons.title: Hallucinations — When AI Makes Things Up
objectives: Define AI hallucination in plain language
Identify when AI output needs to be fact-checked
Apply the verify-before-use rule

lessons.title: Bias in AI
objectives: Explain how AI can reflect societal biases
Recognise bias in AI outputs using a critical lens
Apply this awareness to South African classroom content

lessons.title: Privacy and Data Safety — POPIA for Educators
objectives: Understand what must never be shared with AI tools
Comply with POPIA when using AI in a school context
Protect learner and school data effectively

lessons.title: Academic Integrity — AI and Your Learners
objectives: Develop a classroom AI policy
Redesign assignments so AI use becomes part of learning
Teach learners to use AI responsibly

lessons.title: The Responsibility Checklist
objectives: Apply a five-point responsibility check before using AI-generated content in class
Download and use the responsibility checklist as an ongoing reference

modules.title: Your Prompt Toolkit
modules.subtitle: Reference and Practice Hub
modules.description: Your permanent reference library. Browse 50+ South African educator prompts, search the full AI glossary, and download quick reference cards to keep nearby.

lessons.title: The SA Educator Prompt Library
objectives: Access and use the categorised prompt library
Customise prompts for your own subject and grade

lessons.title: The AI Glossary
objectives: Look up unfamiliar AI terms in plain, teacher-friendly language
Explain key AI concepts to colleagues

lessons.title: PARTS Framework Quick Reference
objectives: Use the PARTS framework as a quick reference without re-reading the full module

modules.title: You Did It!
modules.subtitle: Certification and Next Steps
modules.description: Celebrate your achievement, reflect on your learning journey, and download your certificate of completion.

lessons.title: Your Learning Journey
objectives: Consolidate learning across all modules
Identify two AI habits to build into your weekly practice

lessons.title: Certificate of Completion
objectives: Generate and download your personalised certificate of completion
